title: Top Down View
description: Play Minecraft from a top-down perspective inspired by ARPGs like Path of Exile 2 or Diablo.
body:
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/dLZhsdxUd5k" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Top Down View lets you play Minecraft from a top-down perspective inspired by ARPGs like Path of Exile 2 or Diablo. Experience combat and exploration from a whole new angle.

**Dependencies:** [Embeddium](https://modrinth.com/mod/embeddium)

---
**Controls**

*   Toggle View: F4
*   Rotate Camera: R (direction depends on cursor position)
*   Left side ← Counter-clockwise
*   Right side → Clockwise
*   Bottom ↺ 180° flip
*   Zoom: Left Alt + scroll wheel
*   Free Camera Mode: Configure key in settings, hold to freely rotate camera, double-click to reset angles

---
**Compatibility Notes**

*   **Shaders:** The fade feature is incompatible with most major shaders. Please disable the fade feature when using them together. (Note: Fade appears to work with _MakeUp - Ultra Fast | Shaders_.)
*   **Entity Culling:** Causes entities and block entities to vanish.
*   **Sound Physics Remastered:** Audio occlusion/reverb is calculated from the wrong position due to the detached camera.
*   **Epic Fight:** Set `camera_mode = "ALWAYS_BACK"` in `config/epicfight-client.toml` to prevent camera conflicts.
*   **Flerovium:** Sounds become inaudible when zooming out due to its distant sound culling feature
  
---
**Requirements**

*   Minecraft 1.20.1
*   Forge 47.4.10+
*   [Embeddium](https://modrinth.com/mod/embeddium)
*   Client-side only
